statement.en: GS-Quant: Granular Semantic and Generative Structural Quantization for Knowledge Graph Completion
statement.summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) have shown immense potential in Knowledge Graph Completion (KGC), yet bridging the modality gap between continuous graph embeddings and discrete LLM tokens remains a critical challenge. While recent quantization-based approaches attempt to align these modalities, they typically treat quantization as flat numerical compression, resulting in semantically entangled codes that fail to mirror the hierarchical nature of human reasoning. In this paper, we propose GS-Quant, a novel framework that generates semantically coherent and structurally stratified discrete codes for KG entities. Unlike prior methods, GS-Quant is grounded in the insight that entity representations should follow a linguistic coarse-to-fine logic. We introduce a Granular Semantic Enhancement modul

proposer: Qizhuo Xie, Yunhui Liu, Yu Xing et al.

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: Paper dated 2026-04-25 (future date relative to training cutoff); proposes novel framework (GS-Quant) combining quantization, LLMs, and KGC. Core claims (semantic coherence in discrete codes, coarse-to-fine hierarchical encoding) are theoretically motivated but lack peer-reviewed validation in provided abstract. Status: preprint/provisional under community evaluation.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 実体は粗い語義から細い構造へと、層状に積み重なる言語的な息吹である。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 上方の輝きが細粒化され、層状に降り、最後に離散的な指先へと枝分かれする。
